label: Newborn
whatToExpect: Weight, length, and head circumference measurements
Full physical exam including heart, hips, eyes, and hearing
Check for jaundice (yellowing of the skin)
Discuss feeding patterns — breast or formula, frequency, and output
Umbilical cord care and circumcision care (if applicable)
Review newborn screening test results (heel prick)
Discuss safe sleep practices and tummy time
Check on parent well-being and postpartum recovery

vaccines.name: Hepatitis B (HepB) — 1st dose
vaccines.note: Usually given at the hospital within 24 hours of birth

label: 1 Month
whatToExpect: Weight, length, and head circumference measurements
Physical exam including fontanelles (soft spots), heart, and hips
Discuss feeding — frequency, duration, and any concerns
Assess growth curve and weight gain since birth
Ask about sleep patterns and safe sleep
Check for signs of colic or reflux
Discuss tummy time and early development
Check on parent well-being and postpartum mood

label: 2 Months
whatToExpect: Weight, length, and head circumference measurements
Full physical exam
First big round of vaccinations (multiple shots)
Discuss feeding schedule and any changes
Review developmental milestones
Talk about sleep patterns and routines
Screen for postpartum depression (Edinburgh scale)
Discuss childcare plans if returning to work

Social/Emotional: Smiles at people (social smile)
Looks at you and coos or makes sounds when you talk
Language/Communication: Makes sounds other than crying
Reacts to loud sounds
Cognitive: Watches you as you move
Looks at a toy for several seconds
Physical/Motor: Holds head up when on tummy
Moves both arms and both legs
Opens hands briefly

label: 4 Months
whatToExpect: Weight, length, and head circumference measurements
Full physical exam
Second round of vaccinations
Discuss sleep — this is when the 4-month sleep regression often hits
Review motor milestones (rolling, reaching, grasping)
Talk about readiness signs for solid foods
Discuss safe sleep as baby becomes more mobile
May check for flat spots on head (plagiocephaly)

vaccineLinks.title: How Can You Know If Vaccines Are Safe?
Your Guide to Childhood Vaccinations Before Age 2

Social/Emotional: Smiles on their own to get your attention
Chuckles (not yet a full laugh) when you try to make them laugh
Looks at you, moves, or makes sounds to get or keep your attention
Language/Communication: Makes sounds like 'ooo' and 'aah'
Makes sounds back when you talk to them
Turns head toward the sound of your voice
Cognitive: If hungry, opens mouth when they see breast or bottle
Looks at their hands with interest
Physical/Motor: Holds head steady without support when being held
Holds a toy when you put it in their hand
Uses arm to swing at toys
Brings hands to mouth
Pushes up onto elbows/forearms during tummy time

label: 6 Months
whatToExpect: Weight, length, and head circumference measurements
Full physical exam
Third round of vaccinations
Discuss starting solid foods (if not already started)
Review motor milestones — sitting, reaching, transferring objects
Check for teeth coming in
May do a blood draw to check for anemia and lead
Discuss food allergy introduction (especially peanut, egg)

vaccines.name: Hib — 3rd dose (if needed)
vaccines.note: Depends on brand used for earlier doses

vaccines.name: Rotavirus (RV) — 3rd dose (if needed)
vaccines.note: Depends on brand; some are 2-dose series

vaccines.name: Influenza (Flu) — 1st dose
vaccines.note: If during flu season (2 doses needed first year, 4 weeks apart)

vaccineLinks.title: How Can You Know If Vaccines Are Safe?
Your Guide to Childhood Vaccinations Before Age 2

Social/Emotional: Knows familiar people
Likes to look at self in mirror
Laughs
Language/Communication: Takes turns making sounds with you
Blows 'raspberries' (sticks tongue out and blows)
Makes squealing noises
Cognitive: Puts things in their mouth to explore them
Reaches to grab a toy they want
Closes lips to show they don't want more food
Physical/Motor: Rolls from tummy to back
Pushes up with straight arms when on tummy
Leans on hands to support self while sitting

label: 9 Months
whatToExpect: Weight, length, and head circumference measurements
Full physical exam
Developmental screening questionnaire (ASQ or similar)
Blood test for lead and anemia (if not done at 6 months)
Assess crawling, pulling to stand, and fine motor skills
Discuss feeding — finger foods, self-feeding, cup drinking
Talk about baby-proofing as baby becomes mobile
Review sleep and any ongoing challenges

vaccineLinks.title: How Can You Know If Vaccines Are Safe?
Your Guide to Childhood Vaccinations Before Age 2

Social/Emotional: May be afraid of strangers
May be clingy with familiar adults
Has favorite toys
Language/Communication: Makes different sounds like 'mamamama' and 'babababa'
Lifts arms up to be picked up
Understands 'no' (pauses briefly or stops)
Cognitive: Looks when you call their name
Looks for objects when dropped out of sight (object permanence)
Bangs two things together
Physical/Motor: Gets to a sitting position by self
Moves things from one hand to the other
Uses fingers to rake food towards self
Sits without support

label: 12 Months
whatToExpect: Weight, length, and head circumference measurements
Full physical exam
Several new vaccinations
Blood test for lead and anemia (if not done earlier)
Developmental milestone review — first words, walking, gestures
Discuss transition from formula/breast milk to whole milk
Talk about toddler nutrition and self-feeding
Discuss dental health — first dentist visit recommended by age 1

vaccineLinks.title: How Can You Know If Vaccines Are Safe?
Your Guide to Childhood Vaccinations Before Age 2
Measles 101

Social/Emotional: Plays games with you, like pat-a-cake
Waves 'bye-bye'
Calls a parent 'mama' or 'dada' or another special name
Language/Communication: Understands 'no' (pauses or stops)
Says 1–2 words besides 'mama' and 'dada'
Follows simple directions with gestures (e.g., give me the ball)
Cognitive: Puts something in a container, like a block in a cup
Looks for things they see you hide
Points to ask for things or get help
Physical/Motor: Pulls up to stand
Walks holding on to furniture (cruising)
Drinks from a cup without a lid, with help
Picks things up with thumb and pointer finger (pincer grasp)

suggestedQuestions: How do I transition from formula/breast milk to whole milk?
Should I be worried that my baby isn't walking yet?
How many words should my child be saying?

articles.title: Should I Switch My Child from Whole to 2% Milk?

label: 15 Months
whatToExpect: Weight, length, and head circumference measurements
Full physical exam
Catch-up on any vaccines not yet given
Assess walking (most children walk by 15 months)
Review language development — words, gestures, understanding
Discuss behavior — tantrums are starting!
Talk about toddler eating habits and picky eating
Screen for autism spectrum disorder (M-CHAT or similar)

vaccines.name: Hib — booster (if not given at 12 months)
Hepatitis A (HepA) — 2nd dose
vaccines.note: At least 6 months after the 1st dose

vaccineLinks.title: How Can You Know If Vaccines Are Safe?
Your Guide to Childhood Vaccinations Before Age 2

Social/Emotional: Copies other children while playing (like taking toys out of a container)
Shows you an object they like
Claps when excited
Hugs stuffed doll or other toy
Language/Communication: Tries to say 1–2 words besides 'mama' or 'dada'
Looks at a familiar object when you name it
Follows directions given with both a gesture and words
Points to ask for something or to get help
Cognitive: Tries to use things the right way (phone, cup, book)
Stacks at least 2 small objects
Physical/Motor: Takes a few steps on their own
Uses fingers to feed themselves some food

label: 18 Months
whatToExpect: Weight, length, and head circumference measurements
Full physical exam
Formal autism screening (M-CHAT-R/F) — required at this visit
Developmental screening questionnaire
Review language — should have several words by now
Discuss behavior, independence, and limit-setting
Talk about dental care and brushing teeth
Discuss potty training readiness (not expected yet, but on the horizon)

vaccines.name: DTaP — 4th dose (if not given at 15 months)

vaccineLinks.title: How Can You Know If Vaccines Are Safe?

Social/Emotional: Moves away from you, but looks to make sure you are close by
Points to show you something interesting
Puts hands out for you to wash them
Looks at a few pages in a book with you
Language/Communication: Tries to say 3 or more words besides 'mama' or 'dada'
Follows 1-step directions without any gestures
Points to show you what they want
Cognitive: Copies you doing chores, like sweeping with a broom
Plays with toys in a simple way, like pushing a toy car
Physical/Motor: Walks without holding on to anyone or anything
Scribbles
Drinks from a cup without a lid
Feeds themselves with fingers
Tries to use a spoon

label: 2 Years
whatToExpect: Weight, length, and head circumference measurements
Full physical exam
Formal autism screening (M-CHAT-R/F) — required again at this visit
Developmental and behavioral screening
Review language — should be combining 2 words by now
Discuss potty training, if child shows signs of readiness
Talk about discipline approaches and managing tantrums
Begin plotting BMI on growth charts
May screen for lead exposure based on risk factors

vaccineLinks.title: How Can You Know If Vaccines Are Safe?
Your Guide to Childhood Vaccinations Before Age 2

Social/Emotional: Notices when others are hurt or upset (may pause or cry)
Looks at your face to see how to react in a new situation
Language/Communication: Points to things in a book when you ask
Says at least 2 words together (like 'More milk')
Points to at least 2 body parts when asked
Uses more gestures than just waving and pointing
Cognitive: Holds something in one hand while using the other
Tries to use switches, knobs, or buttons on a toy
Plays with more than one toy at a time
Physical/Motor: Kicks a ball
Runs
Walks (not climbs) up a few stairs with or without help
Eats with a spoon

label: 2.5 Years
whatToExpect: Weight, length, and head circumference measurements
Full physical exam
Developmental screening — focus on language and social skills
Review toilet training progress
Discuss preschool readiness and social development
Talk about managing challenging behaviors
Check dental health — should be seeing a dentist regularly
Review nutrition and any persistent picky eating

vaccines.name: Catch-up vaccines
vaccines.note: Any missed doses from the schedule

vaccineLinks.title: How Can You Know If Vaccines Are Safe?
Your Guide to Childhood Vaccinations Before Age 2

Social/Emotional: Plays next to other children and sometimes plays with them
Shows you what they can do by saying 'Look at me!'
Follows simple routines when told (like picking up toys)
Language/Communication: Says about 50 words
Says 2 or more words together, with one action word (like 'Doggy run')
Names things in a book when you point and ask 'What is this?'
Says words like 'I,' 'me,' or 'we'
Cognitive: Uses things to pretend (feeds a stuffed animal)
Shows simple problem-solving skills (stands on stool to reach something)
Follows 2-step instructions (like 'Put the toy down and close the door')
Physical/Motor: Uses hands to twist things (turns doorknobs, unscrews lids)
Takes some clothes off by themselves
Jumps off the ground with both feet
Turns pages of a book one at a time

label: 3 Years
whatToExpect: Weight, height (standing), and BMI measurements
Full physical exam
Vision screening (first formal screen for many practices)

Review speech and language — should be mostly understandable

Social/Emotional: Calms down within 10 minutes after you leave (like at childcare drop-off)
Notices other children and joins them to play
Language/Communication: Talks with you in conversation using at least 2 back-and-forth exchanges
Asks 'who,' 'what,' 'where,' or 'why' questions
Says what action is happening in a picture (running, eating, playing)
Says first name when asked
Talks well enough for others to understand, most of the time
Cognitive: Draws a circle when you show them how
Avoids touching hot objects (like a stove) when warned
Physical/Motor: Strings items together (like large beads)
Puts on some clothes by themselves (like loose pants or jacket)
Uses a fork
